text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of working on the entire project at once, teams break the work into short, fixed periods called **sprints**, usually lasting one to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning session where the team selects tasks from a **backlog**—a prioritized list of features and fixes. These tasks are often written as **user stories**, brief descriptions of a feature from the user's perspective. During the sprint, the team holds daily **standups**, short meetings where members discuss progress and any blockers. This **iterative development** process allows teams to release parts of the product quickly and adapt based on feedback.
